re/A.name: A
re/A.description: Make \w, \W, \b, \B, \d, \D, \s and \S perform ASCII-only matching instead of full Unicode matching. This is only meaningful for Unicode (str) patterns, and is ignored for bytes patterns. Corresponds to the inline flag (?a). Note The U flag still exists for backward compatibility, but is redundant in Python 3 since matches are Unicode by default for str patterns, and Unicode matching isn’t allowed for bytes patterns. UNICODE and the inline flag (?u) are similarly redundant.
